verses.latin_vulgate: Psalmus David, Jeremiæ.   Super flumina Babylonis illic sedimus et flevimus,  cum recordaremur Sion.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֥ל־נַהֲר֨וֹת ׀ בָּבֶ֗ל שָׁ֣ם יָ֭שַׁבְנוּ גַּם־בָּכִ֑ינוּ בְּ֝זׇכְרֵ֗נוּ אֶת־צִיּֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Upon the rivers of Babylon, there we sat and wept: when we remembered Sion:
verses.finnish_biblia: Babelin virtain tykönä me istuimme ja itkimme, kuin me Zionin muistimme.
verses.king_james: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down, yea, we wept, when we remembered Zion.

verses.latin_vulgate: In salicibus in medio ejus  suspendimus organa nostra :
verses.douay_rheims: On the willows in the midst thereof we hung up our instruments.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kanteleemme me ripustimme pajuihin, jotka siellä olivat.
verses.king_james: We hanged our harps upon the willows in the midst thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia illic interrogaverunt nos, qui captivos duxerunt nos,  verba cantionum ;  et qui abduxerunt nos :  Hymnum cantate nobis de canticis Sion.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י שָׁ֨ם שְֽׁאֵל֪וּנוּ שׁוֹבֵ֡ינוּ דִּבְרֵי־שִׁ֭יר וְתוֹלָלֵ֣ינוּ שִׂמְחָ֑ה שִׁ֥ירוּ לָ֝֗נוּ מִשִּׁ֥יר צִיּֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: For there they that led us into captivity required of us the words of songs. And they that carried us away, said: Sing ye to us a hymn of the songs of Sion.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siellä he käskivät meidän veisata, jotka meitä vankina pitivät, ja iloita meidän itkussamme: Veisatkaat meille Zionin virsiä.
verses.king_james: For there they that carried us away captive required of us a song; and they that wasted us required of us mirth, saying, Sing us one of the songs of Zion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo cantabimus canticum Domini  in terra aliena ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֗יךְ נָשִׁ֥יר אֶת־שִׁיר־יְהֹוָ֑ה עַ֝֗ל אַדְמַ֥ת נֵכָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: How shall we sing the song of the Lord in a strange land?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuinka me veisaisimme Herran veisun vieraalla maalla?
verses.king_james: How shall we sing the Lord’s song in a strange land?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si oblitus fuero tui, Jerusalem,  oblivioni detur dextera mea.
verses.douay_rheims: If I forget thee, O Jerusalem, let my right hand be forgotten.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos minä unohdan sinua, Jerusalem, niin olkoon oikia käteni unohdettu.
verses.king_james: If I forget thee, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget her cunning.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhæreat lingua mea faucibus meis,  si non meminero tui ;  si non proposuero Jerusalem  in principio lætitiæ meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּדְבַּֽק־לְשׁוֹנִ֨י ׀ לְחִכִּי֮ אִם־לֹ֢א אֶ֫זְכְּרֵ֥כִי אִם־לֹ֣א אַ֭עֲלֶה אֶת־יְרוּשָׁלַ֑͏ִם עַ֝֗ל רֹ֣אשׁ שִׂמְחָתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let my tongue cleave to my jaws, if I do not remember thee: If I make not Jerusalem the beginning of my joy.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tarttukoon kieleni suuni lakeen, ellen minä sinua muista, ellen minä tee Jerusalemia ylimmäiseksi ilokseni.
verses.king_james: If I do not remember thee, let my tongue cleave to the roof of my mouth; if I prefer not Jerusalem above my chief joy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Memor esto, Domine, filiorum Edom,  in die Jerusalem :  qui dicunt : Exinanite, exinanite  usque ad fundamentum in ea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זְכֹ֤ר יְהֹוָ֨ה ׀ לִבְנֵ֬י אֱד֗וֹם אֵת֮ י֤וֹם יְֽר֫וּשָׁלָ֥͏ִם הָ֭אֹ֣מְרִים עָ֤רוּ ׀ עָ֑רוּ עַ֝֗ד הַיְס֥וֹד בָּֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Remember, O Lord, the children of Edom, in the day of Jerusalem: Who say: Rase it, rase it, even to the foundation thereof.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra, muista Edomin lapsia Jerusalemin päivänä, jotka sanovat: repikäät maahan hamaan hänen perustukseensa asti.
verses.king_james: Remember, O Lord, the children of Edom in the day of Jerusalem; who said, Rase it, rase it, even to the foundation thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filia Babylonis misera ! beatus qui retribuet tibi  retributionem tuam quam retribuisti nobis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּת־בָּבֶ֗ל הַשְּׁד֫וּדָ֥ה אַשְׁרֵ֥י שֶׁיְשַׁלֶּם־לָ֑ךְ אֶת־גְּ֝מוּלֵ֗ךְ שֶׁגָּמַ֥לְתְּ לָֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: O daughter of Babylon, miserable: blessed shall he be who shall repay thee thy payment which thou hast paid us.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä hävitetty tytär, Babel; autuas on, se joka sinulle kostaa, niinkuin sinä meille tehnyt olet.
verses.king_james: O daughter of Babylon, who art to be destroyed; happy shall he be, that rewardeth thee as thou hast served us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Beatus qui tenebit,  et allidet parvulos tuos ad petram.
verses.douay_rheims: Blessed be he that shall take and dash thy little ones against the rock.
verses.finnish_biblia: Autuas on se, joka piskuiset lapses ottaa ja paiskaa kiviin.
verses.king_james: Happy shall he be, that taketh and dasheth thy little ones against the stones.
